calibrationTable.rationale: Auto-fusion-recipe (47 origins × 20 × 1.8 = 1692) + github-stars-own (34 mag, 34k stars). Single-skill TM dominated by fusion. Diversity: 2 types — but fusion at 47 origins is overwhelmingly S-tier evidence (≥5 origins S-capable per Marco). My stance: relax diversity gate at S to allow ≥2 types when ONE is S-tier evidence with ≥10 origins fusion-recipe. Rationale: 47 components is structural proof, not gameable at that volume. Lands at S=1444.

Auto-fusion-recipe (46 origins × 20 × 1.8 = 1656) + github-stars-own (98.9 mag from 99k stars × 1.0). Diversity=2 types but fusion ≥10 origins satisfies relaxed S-diversity. TM=1755. Genuinely earned — 99k stars + 46 components. The whole point of attainable-S: this skill SHOULD be S today.

github-stars-own capped at 200 (196k stars hit cap) + fusion-recipe (11 origins × 20 × 1.8 = 396). TM=596. ≥10 origins fusion + S-capable stars (Marco rule #1: pure stars CAN reach A; here at 200 mag combined with fusion makes S legitimate). Lower S threshold (200) means 596 is comfortably S.

Migration auto-mints fusion-recipe from suiteComponents (19 × 20 × 1.8 = 684). Empty evidence today; fusion-only gets S under my relaxed diversity rule (≥10 origins fusion = structural S anchor). Without this, all 6 corpus Ultimates would crash to ungraded — unacceptable migration outcome. My stance prioritizes pragmatic continuity.

10 components × 20 × 1.8 = 360 from auto-fusion-recipe. Empty links/empty evidence — this is the phantom-skill edge case. Under attainable-S with fusion ≥10 origins → S-eligible by structure alone. Better outcome than ungraded for migration. Flag for manual evidence review post-migration but don't crash the rank.

Cites parent ruvnet/ruflo (34k stars) — proxy-containment NOT github-stars-own (different owner contextually within suite). 34×0.8=27.2 mag. + auto-fusion-recipe (5 origins × 20 × 1.8 = 180). TM=207, just below S=200... actually 207>200. But fusion at exactly 5 origins is the structural minimum, plus diversity=2 types is borderline. Lands at A under my stance (need ≥10 origins for S-diversity-relaxation OR ≥3 types). Modest demotion 5★→4★ acceptable — agentdb hasn't earned Ultimate via independent evidence.

Mothership-stars discount applied: 99k stars / 46 components = 2.15 mag (NOT 99). + auto-fusion-recipe 2 origins × 20 × 1.8 = 72. TM=74. Drops from 4★ to 3★ Extra. This is deliberate — sub-skills should not all inherit full mothership stars. NEW SUB-RULE I add: when sub-skill cites parent suite repo with same owner, magnitude divides by suiteComponents count of parent. Closes mothership gameability vector explicitly.

Type=basic, NO auto-fusion-recipe per Marco rule #2. Mothership-stars discount: 99/46=2.15. No other evidence. Hard drop to ungraded. Surfaces real corpus-hygiene problem: basic-tier skills cited as 4★ via parent stars only. Migration should flag for benchmark/peer-review evidence collection, not silently retain 4★.

3 repo entries (~30 mag each weight 0.6 = 54 total) + fusion-recipe 5 prereqs × 20 × 1.8 = 180. TM=234. Under attainable-S threshold (200), clears S. Diversity: 2 types but fusion ≥5 origins is the floor for Ultimate per Marco rule #2. My relaxed rule needs ≥10 origins for diversity-bypass — this has 5, so technically caps at A. Reverting: A=234 with 1 A-tier evidence (fusion at 180 is solidly A-tier post-weight). Lands at A, not S. Honest reassessment: A grade, drift up from current B.

Auto-fusion-recipe from prereqs≥5: 31 × 20 × 1.8 = 1116. Massive structural fusion. Under attainable-S diversity-relaxation (≥10 origins fusion → S-eligible single-type), lands at S. This is exactly the corpus case my stance is designed for: huge structural assembly with no external evidence yet — give it S today, contributors can defend with verifier/benchmark later.

5 prereqs × 20 × 1.8 = 180. Below S threshold (200). Single type, only 5 origins so no diversity-bypass. Lands at A. Drops from Ultimate to Apex (5★→4★). Acceptable: minimum-prereq Ultimates without external evidence should earn their S, not be granted it. My stance is pragmatic but not blind.

Backfill scrapes notes for '196k+ stars' → seeds proxy-containment (parent obra/superpowers: 196 × 0.8 = 156.8 cap 160). + repo evidence (~30 mag × 0.6 = 18). + fusion-recipe if any prereqs (assume 2 origins × 20 × 1.8 = 72). TM=246. Under attainable-S (200) with 3 distinct types (proxy-containment, repo-own, fusion-recipe) → clean S. Drift up: legitimate skill that legacy system under-graded due to missing evidence types.

9-row evidence (6 arxiv incl BERT + 3 repo). New formula adds arxiv same-type plateau (1.0/0.5/0.25/0.125/0.0625/0.0625 — I CAP arxiv stack at 5 useful entries to prevent stuffing): BERT=100, A=50, A=25, A=12.5, A=6.25 = 193.75 from arxiv. + 3 repo (proxy-containment plateau): 30+15+7.5=52.5. TM=246 actually (recompute: 193.75+52.5=246). With 2 distinct types but BERT alone is S-tier evidence (citations ≥10k → magnitude 100, weight 1.0 = 100, that's at A-cap not S). Under my rules: 2 types + 1 A-tier evidence × multiple → A grade actually, not S. Corrected: A grade at 246 magnitude. Strong A — promote from basic to extra/unique tier.

5 same-URL repo rows DEDUPED to 1 (my new same-source dedup rule on repo-own). 30 × 0.6 = 18 mag from 1 deduped repo-own. Plus self-attestation if applicable (5×0.5=2.5). TM=20-36. Lands at C/B boundary. Closes the same-source stacking vector explicitly. Skill stays ~3★ Extra but with honest evidence count.

diversityRules.S_grade_diversity_relaxation: ≥3 distinct types REQUIRED for S — UNLESS fusion-recipe has ≥10 origins (then ≥2 types acceptable). This recognizes 10+ origins as structural S-tier evidence on its own merit. Below 10 origins, full ≥3 type diversity required.
diversityRules.A_grade_diversity: ≥1 distinct type, magnitude ≥100, ≥1 A-tier evidence row (unchanged from baseline)
diversityRules.Ultimate_rank_requirement: Hard-requires fusion-recipe evidence (Marco rule #2 preserved). My stance does NOT relax this — fusion is mandatory inherent for Ultimate.

magnitudeAdjustments: fusion-recipe weight increased 1.5 → 1.8 (justification: structural assembly is the most gameability-resistant evidence we have at registry-scale; rewards composability)
S threshold lowered 250 → 200 (justification: with current corpus, 250 leaves only 2-3 skills at S; 200 yields ~30-40% of legitimate 5★+ skills at S, demonstrating attainability without inflation)
A threshold UNCHANGED at 100 (Marco rule #1 calibration — pure-stars can hit A is preserved)
NEW: arxiv same-type plateau added (1.0×/0.5×/0.25×/0.125×/0.0625× max 5 entries, beyond 5 → 0). Closes citation-stuffing vector identified by Surveyor C.
NEW: repo-own same-source dedup — same URL collapses to 1 row before scoring. Closes registry-curation 5x same-URL stacking.
NEW: mothership-stars discount — when sub-skill cites parent suite repo with same owner AND parent has suiteComponents=N, magnitude divides by N. Closes garrytan/* mass-jump vector.
NEW: github-stars-own fork-network dedup — collapse forks to canonical via GitHub fork-of API at scrape time.
fusion-recipe magnitude formula UNCHANGED at 20 × origins (only weight changed)

socialSignalTuning.comment: Marco rule #7 preserved: social-signal hard A-cap at 80 — but I tighten interpretation to 80 TOTAL across all rows (not 80 per row). Closes paraphrase-smurfing across 4 platforms identified by Surveyor C.
socialSignalTuning.creator_multiplier_deterministic: verifier=1.0, named-skill-holder=0.6, anon=0.3, same-as-author=0.0. Removes subjective 'recognized voice' — closes ghostwriter vector.

gameabilityClosed: Mothership-stars (Surveyor A vector #1): sub-skill ÷ suiteComponents-count rule prevents 46 garrytan/* skills from each claiming 99k-star magnitude. Now garrytan/cso scores ~2 from the parent repo, not 99.
Same-URL repo-own stacking (Surveyor C vector #1): registry-curation's 5 identical-URL rows collapse to 1 before scoring. Cannot inflate by citing same repo via different sub-paths.

Arxiv citation cherry-picking (Surveyor C vector #3): new same-type plateau on arxiv (1.0/0.5/0.25/0.125/0.0625, cap at 5 entries) means stacking 3-5 tangentially-related papers cannot reach S magnitude alone — diversity gate then forces external validation.

Cross-platform social-signal smurfing (Surveyor C vector #5): hard-A-cap reinterpreted as 80 magnitude TOTAL across all social rows. 4-platform paraphrase yields 80 max, not 320. Combined with deterministic creator_multiplier (no subjective 'recognized voice'), ghostwriter exploit dies.
Fork-network star inflation (Surveyor C vector #2): GitHub fork-of API check at backfill collapses forks to canonical parent repo. Cannot run 3 mirrors and claim 3 separate github-stars-own rows.

Self-promotion via repo-not-self-attestation (Surveyor B): contributors with origin:true AND same-username GitHub URL get reclassified to self-attestation (5 mag, C-cap), not repo-own (30 mag). Half the named-corpus repo evidence properly downgrades.
gameabilityOpened: Lower S threshold (250→200) reduces buffer against future formula errors. If a future evidence type miscalibrates magnitude high, 200 is reached faster than 250. Mitigation: quarterly grade re-audit required in PR-2.

Fusion-recipe weight increase (1.5→1.8) makes 'fake suiteComponents stuffing' more attractive. A 10-origin trivial-component suite scores 360 mag (fusion alone, S-eligible under my diversity-relaxation). Mitigation: require components themselves to be ≥B grade for the origin to count toward fusion-recipe (cite Surveyor A vector #2 mitigation explicitly).

Diversity-relaxation at ≥10 origins fusion creates incentive to pad component lists to exactly 10. A skill at 9 origins is incentivized to add a 10th low-quality component. Mitigation: gradient instead of cliff — at 10+ origins, allow 2-type S; at 20+ origins, allow 1-type S (pure fusion). Codified in PR-2 if data supports.

Verifier-attestation 'no decay' (Marco rule #4 — I preserve it) combined with a more reachable S means a verifier ring (3 friendly 4★ holders) is now 1 step from S, not 2. Mitigation: require verifier 6-month tenure at 4★ before attestations count, AND null contributions when verifier is demoted. Cite Surveyor C vector #4.
stanceDescription: P2-Attainable-S Pragmatist: Lower S threshold to 200 (from 250) and increase fusion-recipe weight to 1.8 (from 1.5) so genuinely strong skills can earn S immediately at migration time without re-evidencing scrambles. Preserve all Marco hard constraints (Ultimate-fusion mandatory, social hard-A-cap, pure-stars-A reachable, proxy max-3 plateau, verifier no-decay). Add a diversity-gate relaxation: at ≥10 fusion origins, ≥2 types acceptable for S (≥10 origins is itself structural evidence). Close 6 named gameability vectors via dedup, plateau, and discount sub-rules. Accept ~30-50% of current 5★+ skills landing at S immediately as the desired outcome — system shows it works.
tradeoffs: Tradeoff 1 — More S grades, faster, but reduced safety margin: Lowering S=200 means ~30-40% of 5★+ skills land at S at migration vs. ~5% at S=250. Pragmatic win (system demonstrates attainability) but if a future evidence-type miscalibration over-credits magnitude, S inflation arrives faster. Quarterly re-audit becomes mandatory.

Tradeoff 2 — Fusion weight bump rewards structure but punishes solo-research skills: Increasing fusion-recipe 1.5→1.8 means suite-builders (mattpocock/skills, ruvnet/ruflo, garrytan/gstack) jump comfortably to S, while individual-contributor skills doing deep research (e.g., a single-author benchmark-driven skill with no fusion) need more evidence types to reach the same grade. This biases the registry toward composable suite ecosystems — arguably the right cultural signal but explicit.

Tradeoff 3 — Diversity-relaxation at ≥10 origins introduces a cliff: A skill at exactly 10 origins gets S-eligible with 2 types; a skill at 9 origins needs 3 types. This creates a small incentive to pad component lists by 1. Fixable with gradient (e.g., 7 origins = 2.5 types credit) but adds formula complexity. I accept the cliff for v1 simplicity; PR-2 can smooth.

Tradeoff 4 — Mothership-stars discount is unfriendly to legitimate suite sub-skills: garrytan/cso is a serious security capability, not a star-leech. Dividing its magnitude by 46 is harsh — but the alternative (every sub-skill claiming 99k stars) is worse. Sub-skills must earn their grade via repo-own commits/contributors at sub-path level OR verifier attestations OR benchmark wins. Acceptable migration pain.

Tradeoff 5 — Phantom-skill rescue (mattpocock/engineering with 0 evidence) lands at S via auto-fusion-recipe alone: This is uncomfortable. My stance accepts it for v1 because the alternative (catastrophic ungraded drop on 5/6 corpus Ultimates) breaks migration. PR-2 should require auto-minted fusion-recipe evidence to be augmented with at least 1 verifier attestation within 90 days, else the grade automatically demotes to A.
